channel.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive
channel.description: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters
channel.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
channel.itunes:summary: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 24, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: NFX's Pete Flint on "Functional AGI Is Here" and the critical mass theory of startups — why this is the single most important moment for AI founders; The Information's exclusive on how Anthropic fear among investors complicated Cursor's fundraising before its $60B SpaceX acquisition deal, with negative 23% gross margins turning positive and $2.7B ARR; a $290M crypto hack toppling Aave, the largest decentralized lender with $45B in deposits now fallen to $29B; Berkshire Hathaway, Chubb, and Travelers winning state regulatory approval to drop AI coverage from corporate insurance policies; Israeli software M&A activity and the VC secondaries market heating up ahead of mega-IPOs; Google Cloud Next showing enterprises struggling to deploy AI at scale; new security breaches at Anthropic and OpenAI proving Mark Zuckerberg right about open-source AI; PitchBook on VC secondaries market dynamics; and Tesla Q1 earnings showing 16% revenue growth to $22.4B as the company pivots to Robotaxi and Optimus.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: NFX's "Functional AGI Is Here" and the critical moment for AI founders; Cursor's $60B SpaceX acquisition and Anthropic fear among investors; $290M crypto hack toppling Aave; Berkshire Hathaway and Chubb dropping AI insurance coverage; Israeli software M&A trends; Google Cloud enterprise AI deployment challenges; Anthropic and OpenAI security breaches validating open-source AI; PitchBook on VC secondaries; and Tesla Q1 earnings.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 23, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX's $60 billion option to acquire AI coding app Cursor, giving xAI a major foothold in the developer tools market; The Information AI Agenda asking whether OpenAI and Anthropic are quietly moving away from reasoning-heavy models and what that means for the race to AGI; The Information Finance on how CoreWeave seduced the bond market with GPU-backed debt, with shares up 55% this month; Tencent and Alibaba in early talks to invest in DeepSeek at a $20 billion-plus valuation; OpenClaw's open-source growing pains — the fastest-growing AI agent project in history is struggling to balance rapid innovation with enterprise stability as major companies stay stuck on outdated versions; and PitchBook on why some IPOs are roaring back even as the broader market cools.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX's $60B option to buy Cursor AI coding app; OpenAI and Anthropic moving away from reasoning models; CoreWeave seducing the bond market with GPU-backed debt; Tencent and Alibaba in talks to invest in DeepSeek at $20B+; OpenClaw's open-source growing pains balancing innovation and enterprise stability; and PitchBook on the IPO market revival.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 22, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX debt jumped to $23 billion with the confidential IPO prospectus revealing xAI-driven capex at nearly $21B and Starlink generating $7.2B in adjusted EBITDA; Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley's top bankers in "Project Apex" working toward a June IPO — plus SpaceX's $60B option to buy Cursor AI coding tool; Amazon doubling down with up to $25B in Anthropic investment tied to commercial milestones and $100B+ in AWS spending commitments; Anthropic's new government ID verification requirement cutting off Chinese founders from Claude Code; Adobe's shift to outcome-based AI pricing where customers only pay when AI agents complete tasks; the Allbirds GPU pivot — how a failed sneaker brand rebranded as "NewBird AI" and saw its stock surge 582% in a day; the future of software design as AI makes code infinite and taste becomes the differentiator; and Exponential View on the AI jobs paradox — Anthropic staff believe junior engineers can be replaced in 3 months, yet adoption is still lagging at most companies.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX's $23B debt and June IPO plans via Project Apex; Amazon's $25B Anthropic investment with $100B+ AWS commitment; Anthropic's ID verification cutting off Chinese founders from Claude Code; Adobe's outcome-based AI pricing; the Allbirds 582% GPU pivot as NewBird AI; AI's impact on software design and the value of taste; and the AI jobs paradox from Exponential View.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 21, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Elon Musk bought $1.4 billion in SpaceX shares to boost control ahead of the $75B IPO, with a new pay plan worth tens of millions if SpaceX hits $6.6 trillion market cap; AI data center cooling startup Phononic discusses a $1.5 billion+ valuation sale with Lazard; three big questions for Apple's next CEO John Ternus; Google creates a strike team to improve coding models; OpenAI executive backs an AI agent for exhausted data center operators; AI Agenda on OpenAI taking aim at Google with new image models; PitchBook on frontier biotech, agentic AI themes, and private capital fueling a record MLB sale.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Elon Musk bought $1.4B in SpaceX shares ahead of IPO; Phononic AI cooling startup discusses $1.5B+ valuation sale; three big questions for Apple's next CEO John Ternus; Google creates strike team to improve coding models; OpenAI executive backs AI agent for data centers; OpenAI taking aim at Google with new image models; PitchBook on frontier biotech and agentic AI.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 20, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Polymarket in talks to raise $400 million in new funding at a $15 billion valuation (adding to $600M from NYSE parent ICE); China's 10 most important AI chip design firms challenging Nvidia, with Chinese AI chip revenue expected to surge from $2B in 2023 to $82B in 2028 and Nvidia's market share expected to drop to just 8% in China; and Google in talks with Marvell Technology to develop two new AI inference chips — a memory processing unit and a new TPU — as Google diversifies its chip design partners from Broadcom.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Polymarket in talks to raise $400M at a $15B valuation; China's 10 most important AI chip firms challenging Nvidia with $82B revenue projected by 2028; and Google in talks with Marvell Technology to develop new AI inference chips.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 19, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao's power behind the scenes securing $60B in funding and an $800B+ valuation path; SemiAnalysis founder Dylan Patel's rise as Silicon Valley's most influential AI analyst with $100M revenue; Jensen Huang's "electron to token" worldview and Nvidia's orchestration of the AI supply chain; AI employees declining tender offers at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX as IPOs approach; and the math behind why investing in 150 companies beats a 30-company concentrated fund by 2x.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao's power behind the scenes securing $60B in funding and an $800B+ valuation path; SemiAnalysis founder Dylan Patel's rise as Silicon Valley's most influential AI analyst with $100M revenue; Jensen Huang's "electron to token" worldview and Nvidia's orchestration of the AI supply chain; AI employees declining tender offers at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX as IPOs approach; and the math behind why investing in 150 companies beats a 30-company concentrated fund by 2x.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 18, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Dylan Patel and SemiAnalysis grabbing sway in Silicon Valley with exclusive Nvidia praise from Jensen Huang; China's DeepSeek raising outside capital for the first time at a $10B+ valuation; and Flock Safety's $8.4 billion valuation amid protests over police surveillance data sharing with immigration officials.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Dylan Patel and SemiAnalysis grabbing sway in Silicon Valley with exclusive Nvidia praise from Jensen Huang; China's DeepSeek raising outside capital for the first time at a $10B+ valuation; and Flock Safety's $8.4 billion valuation amid protests over police surveillance data sharing with immigration officials.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 17, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information (OpenAI/Cerebras deal, enterprise AI competition), Substack (Project Mythos and JPMorgan financial risk analysis)
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information (OpenAI/Cerebras deal, enterprise AI competition), Substack (Project Mythos and JPMorgan financial risk analysis)

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 16, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information on Apple sending Siri staffers to coding boot camp and AI buying bots reshaping payments; DeepLearning.AI's The Batch on the latest AI research and industry developments; PitchBook's VC and startup funding news; and the AI Agenda newsletter covering the latest in artificial intelligence business and policy.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information on Apple Siri coding boot camp and AI buying bots; DeepLearning.AI The Batch; PitchBook VC/startup news; and the AI Agenda newsletter.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 15, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Azeem Azhar's Exponential View on how armed security guards physically carried OpenAI o3 model weights in locked briefcases to Los Alamos' classified air-gapped Venado supercomputer — revealing how the US is quietly taking control of who gets access to frontier AI; Anthropic's Mythos model and its classified deployment cementing a new era of state-controlled AI diffusion; Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez's masterful Tsinghua University speech using Mateo Ricci's map to pitch EU-China partnership as the world's largest trading relationship amid US-China tension; the Sinica Podcast's Johns Hopkins SAIS conference on what the United States actually wants from China policy, featuring perspectives from former officials; and Brandon Brown's constitutional case for invoking the 25th Amendment as a check on presidential fitness.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Exponential View on armed briefcases carrying OpenAI o3 weights to Los Alamos classified networks and how the US controls frontier AI access; Anthropic's Mythos in classified deployment; Spanish PM Sánchez's Tsinghua speech using Mateo Ricci to pitch EU-China partnership; Sinica Podcast's SAIS conference on what the US wants from China; and a constitutional case for the 25th Amendment.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 14, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX financials show Starlink now drives 61% of revenue and 63% EBITDA margin while rockets burn cash; data labelling startups Handshake and Mercor surge on AI training demand; solid-state battery hype debunked as Chinese and Western carmakers make exaggerated claims; Microsoft's data center strategy unravels — how Amy Hood's spending cuts let Google and Oracle grab key power sites; Fleet's 'Reinforcement Learning Gym' startup reaches $750M valuation with revenues jumping from $1M to $60M annualized in months; OpenAI Stargate executives defect to Meta's new compute unit led by Alexandr Wang; CoreWeave strikes multi-year deal with Anthropic as Sam Altman addresses Molotov cocktail attack on his home; Tesla wins EU driver assistance approval in Netherlands; Hong Kong licenses HSBC and Standard Chartered to issue stablecoins; and Exponential View on how the Middle East conflict is unexpectedly accelerating sustainable aviation fuel adoption.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX Starlink dominates revenue (61%) and EBITDA (63%); Handshake and Mercor surge on AI training demand; solid-state battery hype debunked; Microsoft's data center strategy stumbles as Google and Oracle grab power sites; Fleet's RL Gym startup hits $750M valuation; OpenAI Stargate execs defect to Meta's compute unit; CoreWeave-Anthropic deal; Tesla EU driving software approval; Hong Kong stablecoin licenses; and Exponential View on war-accelerated sustainable aviation fuel adoption.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 13, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Why advanced AI reasoning is locked away — Anthropic gates Claude Mythos for cybersecurity use only as frontier capability moves to coalition-mediated access; Atlassian and Figma lead the enterprise software R&D arms race spending 51% and 97% of revenue on R&D vs. competitors' 19%; Meta's Claudeonomics tokenmaxxing leaderboard rises and falls as employees burn 73 trillion tokens in 30 days; new research vindicates neurosymbolic AI over pure LLMs using NeuroLogic Decoding; and Azeem Azhar on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger, forcing a repricing of critical infrastructure cyber risk.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup: Anthropic gates Claude Mythos for cybersecurity; Atlassian and Figma lead enterprise R&D spending at 51% and 97% of revenue; Meta's Claudeonomics tokenmaxxing leaderboard drama; neurosymbolic AI vindicated over pure LLMs; and Azeem Azhar on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger and forces repricing of critical infrastructure risk.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 12, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI hype hits the physical energy wall as hyperscaler capex outpaces grid capacity; The Information and Substack newsletters dive into systemic shocks, hardcoded AI logic, and what happens when infrastructure can't keep up with compute demand.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup: AI hype hits the physical energy wall as hyperscaler capex outpaces grid capacity; systemic shocks, hardcoded AI logic, and the limits of compute infrastructure.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 11, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Cisco in talks to acquire Israeli AI security startup Astrix for $250-350M to thwart rogue AI agents; how self-taught programmer Boris Cherny built Claude Code into a megahit propelling Anthropic to $30B ARR; SK Hynix and memory chip makers as undervalued AI plays trading at under 5x earnings vs Nvidia's 22x; OpenAI's Stargate data center leaders depart together to start a new company; PitchBook on agentic AI's $24B VC wave and the shift from per-seat to outcome-based pricing; and Exponential View on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger and why critical infrastructure is systemically mispriced.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Cisco in talks to acquire Astrix AI security startup for $250-350M; Claude Code driving Anthropic to $30B ARR and the self-taught programmer behind it; SK Hynix and memory chips as undervalued AI plays; OpenAI Stargate leadership exodus; PitchBook on agentic AI's $24B VC wave and outcome-based pricing; and Exponential View on Claude Mythos fusing AI capability with danger and the mispricing of critical infrastructure cyber risk.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 10, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI Stargate leadership exits as three senior executives depart to start a new company; SpaceX-xAI loses nearly $5 billion last year from AI spending while planning the largest US IPO ever; OpenAI forecasts advertising revenue hitting $102 billion by 2030 — 36% of projected total revenue; Anthropic's Mythos AI model enables exploitation of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in minutes while defenders take days to patch; Meta launches new Spark model and Muse model family focused on personal superintelligence; former a16z GP Anjney Midha raises $1.3 billion for AMP, a new 'grid for AI' venture firm; Chapter's $3 billion AI Medicare broker backed by two vice presidents (Gore and Vance) triples revenue to $100M with just 33 employees; Western EV makers unlikely to benefit from high gas prices while Chinese BYD and Geely surge; Gary Marcus questions whether Anthropic's Mythos announcement was overblown; NFX's Omri Drory on why AI health and longevity is the last great abundance frontier; and the one-person AI company trend with lessons from MEDVi.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI Stargate leadership exits; SpaceX-xAI $5B loss from AI spending ahead of mega-IPO; OpenAI forecasts $102B in ad revenue by 2030; Anthropic's Mythos AI exploiting cybersecurity flaws faster than defenders can patch; Meta launches Spark/Muse AI models; former a16z GP Anjney Midha raises $1.3B for AMP; Chapter's $3B AI Medicare broker backed by two VPs; Western EV makers unable to capitalize on high gas prices; Gary Marcus on Mythos hype; NFX on AI health as the last abundance frontier; and the one-person AI company trend.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 9, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30B ARR overtaking OpenAI in annualized revenue; Nebius in exclusive talks to acquire AI21 Labs; Polygon Labs raising $100M for stablecoin payments platform; Amazon's tough pricing stance causing major brands to pull products; Claude Code GitHub public commits surging 25x in six months; GitButler raises $17M Series A from a16z for AI-powered git client; Intel joins Elon Musk's Terafab $25B solar compute initiative; Anthropic's Claude Mythos zero-day vulnerability hunter showing AI as the best cybersecurity researcher in the world; and The AI Agenda newsletter on software engineers' concerns about AI job displacement.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30B ARR overtaking OpenAI; Nebius in talks to acquire AI21 Labs; Polygon Labs raising $100M for stablecoin payments; Amazon's tough pricing stance causing brand pullouts; Claude Code GitHub commits surging 25x; GitButler $17M Series A from a16z; Intel joins Elon Musk's Terafab; Anthropic's Claude Mythos zero-day vulnerability hunter; and AI job displacement concerns from The AI Agenda.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 8, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30 billion in annualized revenue (up 58% since February) — likely overtaking OpenAI which was at $25B — fueled by a new 3.5-gigawatt TPU deal with Google and Broadcom; OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and CFO Sarah Friar privately diverge on IPO timing, with Friar questioning 2026 readiness while Altman pushed a $600B five-year compute commitment; GitHub experiencing a historic 14x surge in commits from AI agents — from 1B annually to 275M per week — with Claude Code public commits up 25x in six months; Meta employees competing on a 'Claudeonomics' token leaderboard burning 60 trillion tokens in 30 days with top users hitting 281 billion tokens; and The Free Press on the software engineering existential crisis — veterans losing jobs to AI, with under-25 developer employment down nearly 20% since ChatGPT launched.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30B in annualized revenue overtaking OpenAI; OpenAI CEO and CFO diverge on IPO timing; GitHub's 14x AI-agent commit surge; Meta's Claudeonomics tokenmaxxing culture with 60 trillion tokens; and software engineers facing existential job displacement with under-25 employment down 20%.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 7, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's $122B raise as CEO Sam Altman and CFO Sarah Friar diverge on IPO timing with Friar privately questioning 2026 readiness amid $600B compute commitments; Claude Code and Codex learning peaceful co-existence as Cursor enables parallel multi-agent coding; TensorWave and AMD challenging Nvidia's CUDA moat at Beyond Summit while Stanford's AI infrastructure course becomes 'Compute Coachella'; Meta employees competing on an internal 'Claudeonomics' leaderboard for 'Token Legend' status with 60 trillion tokens burned in 30 days; SpaceX Starlink Mobile's uphill battle — T-Mobile deal worth only ~$100M while Starlink Mobile faces competition from AST SpaceMobile; Honeywell's Laila hybrid electric autonomous aircraft for drone-killing Pentagon missions; Andrew Yang on Oracle's 30K layoffs as data centers replace human salaries; the AI capacity trap — Jevons paradox driving tokens/minute from 6B to 15B in five months; The Adaptive Economy on old world cracking (Brent crude $112, Iran/Strait of Hormuz) while builders rise (Terafab $25B solar compute, Artemis II lunar mission, TSMC $65B Arizona fabs); and Baltimore's Equitech startup ecosystem refusing to wait for permission.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI CEO and CFO diverge on IPO timing; Claude Code and Codex learning co-existence; TensorWave challenging Nvidia's CUDA moat; Meta's 'Claudeonomics' tokenmaxxing leaderboard; SpaceX Starlink Mobile's uphill battle; Honeywell's drone-killing eVTOL aircraft; Andrew Yang on Oracle layoffs as data centers replace humans; the AI capacity trap and Jevons paradox; old world cracking vs. builders rising (Terafab, Artemis II, TSMC); and Baltimore's Equitech startup ecosystem.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 6, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI CEO and CFO diverge on IPO timing as Sarah Friar questions readiness amid $600B compute commitments and $200B+ cash burn; the vibe coding revolution driving an 84% surge in Apple App Store new apps in Q1 2026; the Medvi fraud story — how the NYT glorified a supposed $1.8B solo-founder AI company that turned out to be built on spam; and Scott Alexander's Astral Codex Ten open thread on AI safety, the PauseAI protest, and EA grants.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI CEO and CFO diverge on IPO timing; vibe coding driving 84% surge in App Store apps; the Medvi $1.8B AI fraud story; and ACX Open Thread on AI safety and EA grants.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 5, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The AI compute crunch as OpenAI's CFO admits passing on opportunities due to lack of compute, Codex scaling from 100K to 2M developers, Anthropic tightening session limits, H100 rental prices at 18-month highs, and Alibaba closing-sourcing Qwen; Economists forecasting AI adding 1-1.5% to annual US GDP growth by 2050 but with 10M fewer jobs; Agents reshaping markets with new efficiency gains and regulatory challenges; China's Five-Year Plans shifting from leapfrog strategy to geopolitical contest, rising in robotics with the world's first fully automated humanoid production line; Energy as economic destiny with UK wage stagnation from energy costs, agri-voltaic advances, and the Iran conflict accelerating EV two-wheelers; and notable morsels including Ukraine's drone production scaling from 2K to 4M/year, AI mirage reasoning on visual benchmarks, and chess grandmasters playing suboptimal moves to escape AI-memorized lines.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The AI compute crunch and its implications; AI's economic impact forecasts; AI agents reshaping markets; China's technological rise in robotics; Energy as economic destiny; and notable morsels from Ukraine's drone surge to AI mirage reasoning.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 4, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Claude Code's source code leak revealing 512,000 lines and hidden features like Kairos always-on background agent and undercover git mode; OpenAI shutting down Sora video generator (losing $1M/day) ending its Disney partnership; Google's Lyria 3 music generator rolling out to 750M+ Gemini users with full copyright licensing; LLM inference breakthrough (TTT-E2E) enabling constant-time processing of 128K-token contexts; DeepSeek's V4 model designed for Huawei chips as Chinese tech giants order 100K+ units, raising Ascend 950PR prices 20%; Blackstone acquiring 49% stake in Rowan Digital Infrastructure at $3.8B valuation; OpenAI acquiring tech podcast TBPN ($60M revenue run rate); Pentagon appealing judge's order against Anthropic blacklisting; Amazon curbing misleading Prime Day markdowns; Jay Edelson's AI copyright litigation campaign including new ChatGPT stalker case; Tesla Q1 deliveries up 6% YoY at 358K vehicles; Microsoft releasing MAI AI model suite; Amazon in talks to acquire Globalstar; and Salesforce Agentforce exec departing for rival Sierra.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Claude Code's source code leak and hidden features; OpenAI shutting down Sora; Google's Lyria 3 music generator; LLM inference breakthrough (TTT-E2E); DeepSeek V4 on Huawei chips; Blackstone acquiring Rowan data center firm; OpenAI buying TBPN podcast; Pentagon appealing Anthropic blacklisting; Amazon Prime Day pricing reforms; Jay Edelson's AI litigation campaign; Tesla Q1 deliveries; Microsoft MAI models; Amazon-Globalstar talks; and Salesforce Agentforce exec departure.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 3, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Supabase in talks for $10 billion valuation (double its fall 2025 raise), Anthropic acquires AI biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million to expand its healthcare life sciences group, Blackstone extends the data center buying spree with a $3.8B stake in Rowan Digital Infrastructure, Tether makes a final push for fundraising at a $500 billion valuation, OpenAI's Fidji Simo acquired the TBPN podcast amid a campaign against internal 'side quests', Anthropic's upcoming 'Mythos' model signals a new era of AI cybersecurity risks where AI is now essentially the best cybersecurity researcher in the world, Wrtn Technologies' anime AI storytelling apps hitting $100 million in annual sales, private credit market turmoil at Blackstone and Apollo, the PauseAI protest march to AI company HQs in San Francisco, and Azeem Azhar's AutoBeta bringing autoresearch loops to everyday knowledge work.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Supabase in talks for $10B valuation, Anthropic acquires Coefficient Bio for $400M, Blackstone extends data center buying spree, Tether's $500B fundraising push, OpenAI's Fidji Simo and the TBPN podcast acquisition, Anthropic's 'Mythos' model and new AI cybersecurity risks, Wrtn Technologies' anime AI storytelling hitting $100M annual sales, private credit market turmoil, the PauseAI protest in San Francisco, and autoresearch as the next frontier in knowledge work.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 2, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenRouter nearing $1.3B valuation as the multi-model API layer reaches $50M ARR; Amazon's Rufus AI chat ads yielding data but only 88 clicks vs 500,000 for traditional ads; Anthropic's Claude Code source code leak revealing upcoming 'Kairos' always-on background agent with dream mode and proactive initiative; OpenAI raising $122B at $830B valuation with SoftBank and Nvidia committing $30B each; prediction markets boom as crypto crashes — Kalshi doubles to $22B while Bitcoin halves from $126K to $63K; Gary Marcus on why AI job displacement isn't imminent (Fortune article); China's improving economic sentiment with $13T in household savings ready to deploy (Sinica/Rothman); Adam Tooze on China's renewable energy revolution potentially bringing global CO2 to peak now; and The Free Press on Jacob Siegel's book about censorship in the information age being itself censored.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenRouter nearing $1.3B valuation; Amazon's Rufus AI chat ads yielding data but few sales; Anthropic's Claude Code leak revealing 'Kairos' always-on background agent; OpenAI raising $122B at $830B valuation; prediction markets boom as crypto crashes; Gary Marcus on AI job displacement timeline; China's improving economic sentiment and green energy leadership; Adam Tooze on China's renewable energy revolution; and censorship in the information age.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 1, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX's $75 billion IPO and Nasdaq's new rule change that will force passive fund managers to buy in immediately after listing; AI guardian agents from ServiceNow, Wayfound, and others emerging to monitor rogue AI agents at hedge funds and enterprises; Sequoia Capital naming Doug Leone as chairman after two years; AI disruption pushing software syndicated bank debt prices down 7%; LTX's innovative open-source video generation model running on a laptop; the extraordinary story of Adam — a 3'11" robot training to win the 2028 Olympics in LA; Gary Marcus on AI helping with military operations but failing at strategy in the Iran war; Ethan Mollick on why the chatbot interface paradigm fails knowledge workers and what specialized interfaces like Claude Dispatch mean for the future; and Lorenzo Thione on Google's TurboQuant breakthrough cutting the cloud leash for frontier AI models.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX's $75B IPO and Nasdaq rule change forcing passive fund managers to buy in; AI guardian agents monitoring rogue AI from ServiceNow, Wayfound, and others; Sequoia Capital naming Doug Leone as chairman; software debt market turmoil from AI disruption; LTX's open-source laptop video AI; Adam the robot training for the 2028 Olympics; Gary Marcus on AI strategy failures in the Iran war; Ethan Mollick on AI interfaces and Claude Dispatch; and TurboQuant breaking the cloud leash for local AI.
